creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, Dec 23, 2008 at 02:40:03PM +0000, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/129733; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Eygene Ryabinkin <rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru>
> To: Wesley Shields <wxs@FreeBSD.org>
> Cc: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Subject: Re: ports/129733: net-mgmt/nagios-geom: incorrect GEOM status
> 	report from check_geom nagios plugin
> Date: Tue, 23 Dec 2008 17:35:39 +0300
> 
>  Wesley,
>  
>  Tue, Dec 23, 2008 at 09:29:09AM -0500, Wesley Shields wrote:
>  > On Tue, Dec 23, 2008 at 09:40:55AM +0300, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
>  > > I will patch more machines today and will watch for their behaviour.
>  > > More testing is always welcome, but I think that at Wednesday the patch
>  > > could be commited if no one will discover anything very bad.
>  > 
>  > I'll be traveling all day Wednesday and will likely not be able to
>  > commit this until Monday.  I'll try and get to it as soon as I return.
>  
>  In principle, all my patched machines are stable already for a day, so
>  you can patch the port now.  Or leave for Monday -- as you prefer.

The more I look at your patch the more I think you're going about it the
wrong way.  There is a check_geom-1.3.tar.gz file available at the
master site.  We should be using that rather than keeping a local copy
of the script.  The attached patch addresses this fact.  Do you approve?

-- WXS
